title: Print-ready bilingual one-page handouts for walk-in distribution
context: Benefits-Navigator is an openly-licensed corpus of plain-language, primary-source-cited, multilingual, accessible guides to public-assistance programs (SNAP, Medicaid/CHIP, WIC, TANF, LIHEAP, SSI, housing, EITC, Lifeline/ACP), delivered as a static site, printable PDFs, and structured data with an optional retrieval-grounded explainer. It is a HIGH risk-tier project: it provides information, not legal/benefits advice, never makes an individualized eligibility determination, and always routes people to the official application and free human help. No partner, credentialed reviewer, or requestor is yet secured. Future work: print-ready bilingual one-page handouts for walk-in distribution at food banks and libraries, derived from the cited content so figures and citations stay traceable, with the not-advice framing and route-to-human pointers preserved.
objective: Design print-ready bilingual one-page handouts for walk-in distribution, derived from the cited content with not-advice framing and route-to-human pointers preserved.
acceptanceCriteria: Handouts are derived from the cited content so every figure remains traceable to its primary source; the bilingual pairing uses a reviewer-approved translation
Each handout carries the fixed not-advice + non-partisan framing, the official application link, and free human help pointers
Print layout is accessible and legible for walk-in distribution at food banks/libraries; figures kept current via the staleness fail-safe
